mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in 70% of observed cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 205.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which immediately supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 increases are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in 65% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing downturn that reduces capacity utilization and corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).
